reasoning:
To predict the probability of a Binance 1-minute candle hitting a low of $105K or lower before the end of August, we must evaluate the current spot price, distance to barrier, and remaining timeframe. As of August 17th, BTC is trading around $118.3K, having steadily recovered from a temporary local low of ~$112K in early August. 

On August 7th (with 24 days left), Bitcoin was trading around $115K and the market placed a 25% probability on this $105K barrier touch. Since then, the remaining time has been cut by roughly 40% (down to 14.5 days) while the distance required to hit the barrier has expanded from ~8.7% to ~11.3%. 

Using standard barrier option pricing logic (e.g., Brownian motion hit probability proportional to 2 * N(-z)), we can infer the market's implied Z-score on August 7th was approximately 1.15. Because the distance has increased by ~30% and standard deviation of moves has decreased by ~23% (due to less time), the required Z-score has swelled to nearly 1.97. Statistically, this corresponds to roughly a 4.5% to 5% touch probability under normal volatility assumptions. 

To account for standard crypto fat tails and the localized idiosyncratic risk of this specific contract—Binance 1-minute 'scam wicks' and liquidation cascades (which Binance has a history of)—an upward bump is prudent. Expanding from the ~5% baseline, I finalize the probability at 6%.
